# Instagram Post — OpenAI Closes $122B Funding at $852B Valuation, Kills Sora, Pivots to Enterprise
# Week: Week of 2026-03-30
# Brand Voice: GYanTech AI — Visionary, executive audience, lead generation
# Status: Review before posting
# Auto-generated: 2026-04-05T23:02:30.015Z

---

$122 billion raised. Flagship product killed the same week.

OpenAI just closed the largest private funding round in history — $122 billion at an $852 billion valuation. Then it shut down Sora, its video generation tool, after daily costs hit $1 million while users dropped from 1 million to under 500,000.

The signal is impossible to misread. OpenAI is abandoning the "do everything" model and going all-in on enterprise. Coding tools. Agent workflows. An integrated AI Super App bundling ChatGPT, Codex, browsing, and autonomous agents into a single platform.

For business leaders, this is a strategic inflection point. The most capitalized AI company on the planet just told the market that depth wins over breadth. If you are building AI infrastructure around broad tools that might not exist in a year, your architecture needs to account for that risk.

The companies that win the next phase will be the ones that designed for platform shifts — not the ones that assumed stability.

GYanTech AI builds enterprise AI strategies for exactly this kind of volatility. Link in bio.

## Fact-check notes
- OpenAI announced completion of $122 billion in funding with a post-money valuation of $852 billion on March 31, 2026 (Source: Amiko Consulting weekly roundup, citing OpenAI announcement)
- OpenAI terminated Sora after users declined from ~1 million at peak to under 500,000, with the product consuming approximately $1 million per day in costs (Source: Reuters via Amiko Consulting)
- OpenAI is reallocating resources toward coding and enterprise tools in response to competition from Google and Anthropic (Source: Reuters via Amiko Consulting)
